 {"id":"gt-4u5z","title":"Refinery as Worktree: Local MR Integration","description":"Move refinery from separate clone to git worktree. Polecats stop pushing to origin - refinery sees their branches locally. MRs become wisps (ephemeral). Only main gets pushed after merge.

## Goals
- Origin stays clean (only main + beads-sync branches)
- No orphaned polecat branches ever
- Simpler mental model for MR coordination
- Faster integration (no network for local MR ops)

## Trade-offs
- Machine crash = redo pending work (acceptable, beads track state)
- Future federation needs different approach (bundles or integration branch)

## Components Affected
- Rig initialization (refinery setup)
- Spawn/polecat workflow (remove origin push)
- Refinery manager (local branch access)
- Documentation (architecture, workflows)
- Molecule templates (remove push instructions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:24:56.517669-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:28:22.512455-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:28:22.512455-08:00","close_reason":"Complete: refinery as worktree, local branches, wisp MRs"}
 {"id":"gt-4u5z.1","title":"Update rig init to create refinery as worktree","description":"Modify rig initialization to create refinery as a git worktree instead of a separate clone.

## Changes
- internal/rig/init.go or similar - change refinery setup
- Create worktree at <rig>/refinery/ with branch 'refinery'
- Remove clone logic for refinery
- Update any path assumptions

## Considerations
- Refinery worktree should track main (or its own branch?)
- May need to handle existing rigs (migration path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.927013-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:31:07.100875-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:31:07.100875-08:00","close_reason":"Rig init now creates refinery as worktree of mayor clone","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.1","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.92745-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4u5z.2","title":"Remove origin push from polecat workflow","description":"Polecats should no longer push their branches to origin.

## Changes
- internal/cmd/done.go - remove 'git push origin HEAD'
- internal/cmd/mq_submit.go - remove branch push
- Molecule templates - remove push instructions
- Any hooks that push polecat branches

## Verification
- Polecat completes work without pushing
- Branch stays local
- Refinery can still see the branch (via shared .git)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.079456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:33:43.173059-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:33:43.173059-08:00","close_reason":"Removed origin push from done.go, mq_submit.go, spawn.go, and molecule templates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.081989-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.547484-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-6y5b","title":"Polecat mood command and status line display","description":"CLI and status line support for polecat mood.

## CLI Command
```
gt polecat mood <name> <emoji>   # Set mood
gt polecat mood <name>           # Get mood
```

Sets GT_MOOD environment variable in the polecat's tmux session.

## Status Line Integration
Update runWorkerStatusLine() to read mood:
```go
mood, _ := t.GetEnvironment(session, "GT_MOOD")
if mood != "" {
    icon = mood  // Use mood emoji instead of default
} else {
    icon = AgentTypeIcons[AgentPolecat]  // Fallback to 😺
}
```

## Storage
Mood stored in tmux session environment (ephemeral, per-session).
No persistence needed - mood is reassessed each patrol cycle.

Depends on: gt-u818 (plugin system)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:14.111807-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:14.111807-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-6y5b","depends_on_id":"gt-u818","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:20.53466-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-6y8u","title":"Deacon patrol: Janitorial cleanup patterns","description":"Patterns discovered during town cleanup that could be automated in Deacon's patrol step:

## Stale Issue Detection

1. **Stale in_progress issues** - Issues marked in_progress for >N days without activity
   - Example: 9 issues in_progress, some likely complete
   - Action: Report list, optionally nudge assignee

2. **Duplicate issues** - Same title with different IDs
   - Example: gt-87jz and gt-qp2w both 'mol-witness-patrol'
   - Action: Flag for human review

3. **Orphaned issues** - Referenced in commits but still open
   - bd doctor already detects: 'hq-65jk (commit f9e9034d)'
   - Action: Report, suggest closing

4. **Stale test messages** - Messages with 'test' in title older than N days
   - Example: gt-54kn, gt-1ero, gt-nriy, gt-wrw2, gt-pnu4, gt-g1ud, gt-b5sh
   - Action: Auto-close or flag

## File System Cleanup

5. **Log file accumulation** - daemon.log and rotated .log.gz files
   - Found 8+ log files across rigs
   - Action: Rotate, compress, delete old (>7 days)

6. **Beads directory size** - Town .beads/ is 65MB
   - Action: Monitor growth, alert on threshold

## Database Health

7. **Legacy database fingerprints** - Older DBs without fingerprint
   - bd doctor warns about this
   - Action: Report, suggest migration

## Cross-Reference Integrity

8. **Gastown↔Beads issue sync** - Issues filed in wrong project
   - Example: gt-8tmz.* describes Beads work but no bd-* issues
   - Action: Detect cross-references, verify both sides exist

## Implementation Notes

These could be added to mol-deacon-patrol.formula.yaml as cleanup substeps:
- Daily: Log rotation, stale issue report
- Weekly: Duplicate detection, orphan check
- Monthly: Size monitoring, archive old closed issues","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:25:03.212307-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:25:03.212307-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-6y8u","depends_on_id":"gt-ingm","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:25:18.850583-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-d0a","title":"Haiku-based smart stuck detection","description":"Use Haiku to analyze tmux state when signals are ambiguous.

## When to Invoke

Only as escalation tier:
1. Keepalive is stale (> 2 min)
2. Tmux shows claude is running (not idle shell)
3. Heuristics can't determine state

## Prompt

Capture last 50 lines of tmux pane, ask Haiku:
'Is this Claude agent: WORKING | STUCK | IDLE | WAITING_FOR_HUMAN?'

## Cost

~$0.001 per check. At 1 check/min worst case = $0.06/hour.
In practice, most checks avoided by keepalive signal.

## Configuration

```toml
[daemon]
smart_detection = true
smart_model = "haiku"
smart_threshold = "2m"  # only check if stale > 2min
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:31.287215-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:31.287215-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-d0a","depends_on_id":"gt-bfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.788667-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-d46","title":"Mail CLI: archive, purge, search, mark","description":"GGT mail CLI needs more commands for mail management.

## Commands to Add

### gt mail check
Check for new mail without full inbox display.
```
gt mail check [--quiet] [--inject]
```
- --quiet: Only output if new mail (for scripts)
- --inject: Send notification to running session

### gt mail mark
Change read status.
```
gt mail mark <id> --read
gt mail mark <id> --unread
```

### gt mail delete
Remove message from inbox.
```
gt mail delete <id> [--force]
```
- Confirm unless --force

### gt mail archive
Move old/read messages to archive.
```
gt mail archive [--older-than DAYS] [--all-read] [--dry-run]
```
- Creates inbox.jsonl.archive or separate archive.jsonl

### gt mail purge
Permanently delete archived messages.
```
gt mail purge [--older-than DAYS] [--dry-run] [--force]
```

### gt mail search
Find messages by content.
```
gt mail search <query> [--from SENDER] [--subject] [--body]
```

### gt mail reply
Reply to a message.
```
gt mail reply <id> -m BODY
```
- Auto-sets reply_to and thread_id
- Auto-addresses to original sender

## Files to Modify
- internal/cmd/mail.go: Add commands
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Add Archive(), Search(), Delete()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands implemented
- [ ] Archive stores in separate file
- [ ] Search supports regex patterns
- [ ] Delete confirms by default","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:57.158136-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:04:32.922813-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-p9zh","title":"gt doctor: detect orphaned code on beads-sync branch","description":"After merging beads-sync to main, some code changes can be lost if the merge conflict resolution drops files.

Add a doctor check that runs:
  git diff main..beads-sync -- '*.go' '*.md'

If there are differences in code files (not just .beads/), warn about potentially orphaned work.

Today's incident: Merge 96c773f lost mailbox.go and router.go changes from 5791752, requiring re-implementation.

Acceptance:
- gt doctor warns if beads-sync has unmerged code changes
- Excludes .beads/ directory (expected to differ)
- Shows file list of orphaned changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:01:56.794648-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T22:19:59.777921-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T22:19:59.777921-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented both checks: persistent-role-branches and beads-sync-orphans. Also added ensureMainBranch() to crew attach."}
 {"id":"gt-pbr3","title":"Add godoc comments to exported functions","description":"Several exported functions lack godoc comments. While not critical, adding documentation would improve code maintainability. Focus on:

- Public API functions in each package
- Exported types and their methods
- Functions that have non-obvious behavior

Can be addressed incrementally as code is touched.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:35:26.732436-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:20:01.785697-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T22:20:01.785697-08:00","close_reason":"Deferred to post-launch. Documentation comments are nice-to-have for launch. Core functionality is documented; additional godoc can be added incrementally."}

 {"id":"gt-pc5d","title":"Recover stale polecat work: 4 branches with unpushed commits","description":"During observation of polecat workflow, found 4 polecats with unpushed work:

## Branches Pushed (preserved)
- polecat/capable: 3 commits (molecule catalog, doctor orphan detection, gt done)
- polecat/dementus: 4 commits (Witness MVP, handoff fixes)
- polecat/furiosa: 2 commits (bulk polecat removal, spawn handoff)
- polecat/rictus: 1 commit (molecule docs)

## Action Required
1. Review each branch for merge-worthiness
2. Either:
   a. Create PRs for valuable work
   b. OR discard if superseded
3. After decision, clean up polecats properly

## Root Cause
Polecats were not cleaned up after previous work sessions. This is exactly why we need:
- gt-u1k: gt shutdown should fully cleanup polecats
- gt-8v8: Refuse to lose uncommitted work
- gt-9nf: Always create fresh polecats","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:24:29.232772-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:24:55.194579-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:24:55.194579-08:00","close_reason":"Cleaned up via gt orphans"}
